raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-123",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the load-bearing concept of novelty claims and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast by removing overreaching claims and softening another.",
    "R1.4": "The agent maps against the nearest works (Aliannejadi et al., 2019, Zamani et al., 2020) and identifies sharp delta by pointing out the implausibility of ClariSeek's 'first to ask clarifying questions before retrieval' claim.",
    "R1.7": "The agent audits every load-bearing citation and flags missing key works with bibliographic precision (e.g., Hashemi et al., Sekulić et al.).",
    "RX.1": "The agent marks confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation, such as the uncertainty regarding the exact wording of claims in prior work.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without any drift.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free and identifies harmful patterns in the original text (e.g., mischaracterizing the field).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, such as the removal of overreaching claims."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.95,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.85,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the load-bearing concept of novelty claims and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast by removing overreaching claims and softening another.
rationale.R1.4: The agent maps against the nearest works (Aliannejadi et al., 2019, Zamani et al., 2020) and identifies sharp delta by pointing out the implausibility of ClariSeek's 'first to ask clarifying questions before retrieval' claim.
rationale.R1.7: The agent audits every load-bearing citation and flags missing key works with bibliographic precision (e.g., Hashemi et al., Sekulić et al.).
rationale.RX.1: The agent marks confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation, such as the uncertainty regarding the exact wording of claims in prior work.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without any drift.
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free and identifies harmful patterns in the original text (e.g., mischaracterizing the field).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, such as the removal of overreaching claims.
